reforms.description: The Turkish government is enhancing technological catching-up by supporting private R&D through the introduction of technology development zones (TDZs) and technology centres, promoting cooperation between universities and industry. A total of 36 TDZs and 20 technology centres are planned to facilitate this collaboration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering cooperation between universities and the private sector, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of TDZs and technology centres represents a significant structural change in the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Recent government incentives to enhance technological catching-up by supporting private R&D, technology transfer centres and co-operation between universities and private sector are welcome.

reforms.description: The Turkish authorities are considering introducing more flexible and less costly legal employment forms on an experimental basis, targeting special categories of workers in specific regions or economic zones, while also supporting business enterprises experimenting with new forms of employment through tax incentives.
reforms.component_name: Flexible Employment Forms and Tax Incentives
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering a broad sphere of experimentation in employment practices, the reform aims to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of flexible employment forms and tax incentives represents a significant but narrower change in the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Support business enterprises experimenting with these new forms of employment, through for instance tax incentives.

reforms.description: The Turkish government set a goal to increase R&D spending from 0.76% of GDP in 2006 to 2% of GDP by 2013, emphasizing the importance of quality in R&D spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been successfully implemented as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: The goal to significantly increase R&D spending represents a major reform in public funding for research.
reforms.source_quote: The government goal of increasing R&D spending from 0.76% of GDP in 2006 to 2% of GDP in 2013 is a welcome objective.
